summary: Fiery ’79 show with epic Estimated Prophet→Eyes of the World & tight Shakedown Street
blurb: The second set kicks up a notch with an explosive Estimated Prophet into a marathon Eyes of the World, while the first set delivers a tight Shakedown Street → Samson & Delilah → High Time groove. Jerry’s guitar is on fire and Brent’s keyboards add distinct color throughout. The performance rounds out with a rousing U.S. Blues encore.
review:
On November 29, 1979 at Cleveland Public Hall the Dead turned in a high‑octane fall run that still feels fresh for listeners today. The first set opened strong with an upbeat Alabama Getaway and a crisp Mexicali Blues before launching into a searing Shakedown Street that seamlessly segued into Samson & Delilah and High Time – a sequence reviewers call “tight” and full of punchy interplay. The second half truly ignites: Estimated Prophet rolls straight into a pre‑drum run of Eyes Of The World that stretches beyond twenty minutes, showcasing Jerry ripping his guitar with fast‑driven noodling while Brent Mydland’s keyboards weave bright, distinct textures. Phil Lesh sits upfront and center, driving the train with deep, articulate bass, and the rhythm section locks in solidly despite a stumble on Around & Around. The night caps off with an exuberant U.S. Blues encore that leaves the audience humming, cementing this show as one of the standout performances from the band’s late‑70s era.

Across all available soundboard sources the mix is crystal clear – especially the Miller recording (gd1979-11-29.sbd.miller.95831.sbeok.flac16) which captures every nuance, from the crisp cymbals to the warm depth of Phil’s bass and Brent’s synth tones. Whether you’re chasing the monster Eyes Of The World or just want a solid snapshot of the Dead’s fiery late‑fall energy, this show delivers it all in superb audio quality.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All five recordings are marked positive, repeatedly praising the Estimated Prophet→Eyes of the World transition, the tight Shakedown Street jam, and Jerry’s fiery guitar work. Multiple sources highlight Brent's distinct keyboard tone and Phil's upfront bass. No negative sentiment appears across analyses, just a single note about a weak Around & Around, so a strong positive adjustment is warranted.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard mix that captures the full energy of the set, with excellent instrument separation and no drop‑outs.

key_highlights: Epic Estimated Prophet → Eyes Of The World transition with a pre‑drum run.
Killer Shakedown Street → Samson & Delilah → High Time sequence.
Rousing U.S. Blues encore that ends the night on a high note.
Crystal‑clear SBD mix highlighting Brent’s keyboards and Phil’s bass.

band_performance.Jerry: Ripping his guitar with fast‑driven noodling, especially on Eyes Of The World and the U.S. Blues encore.
band_performance.Phil: Upfront and driving the train; bass is clear, deep, and central to the mix.
band_performance.Brent: Distinct keyboard voice that adds color and chemistry with Jerry throughout the jams.
